briefs.title: Burnaby to Replace 1965 Zoning Bylaw with 2026 Version
briefs.summary: Burnaby is getting a new zoning bylaw. The city plans to replace the old 1965 bylaw with an updated version in 2026. This includes changes to development permits and zoning districts. The goal is to modernize city planning and development rules.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed 2026 bylaw and related documents to understand how it affects your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
1.	Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 2026-04-28_.pdf
3.	Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
4.	Attachment 3 - Proposed Streamside Protection and Enhancement Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
5.	Attachment 4 - Proposed Tenant Protection Development Permit Area Guidelines 2026-04-28.pdf
6.	Attachment 5 - Proposed Zone Reassignment Reference Maps 2026-04-28.pdf
7.	Attachment 6 - Rental Replacement and Inclusionary Requirements Evaluation 2026-04-28.pdf
8.	Attachment 7 - Example Inclusionary and Rental Replacement at Occupancy and Second Tenancy 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965
and replace it with the proposed
Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Seeks Approval for Year 2 Funding
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has requested approval from the City Council for its Year 2 operating funding. This funding will support the BHA's operations for the upcoming year.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
1.	BHA 2026 Operating Funding 2026-04-28_.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - BHA Assistance Request for Year 2 Operating Funds 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2026 Property Tax Rates to be Approved
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is being asked to approve the 2026 property tax rates. These rates cover municipal services, regional services, and business improvement areas (BIA).
briefs.action_required: Review the 2026 property tax rates to understand potential impacts on your business.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
1.	2026 Property Tax rates 2026-04-28__.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026_2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

briefs.title: Approving Officer Appointment Requires Council Approval
briefs.summary: The council needs to approve the appointment of an Approving Officer. This officer handles land development and subdivision approvals. This ensures proper oversight of these important decisions.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
1.	Appointment of Approving Officer 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Variance for Retaining Wall Heights on 11th Avenue
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property on 11th Avenue. This permit allows the property owner to build retaining walls that are higher than what's normally allowed by city rules. A Development Variance Permit (DVP) lets a property owner get exceptions to certain zoning rules.
briefs.action_required: Review the approved plans if you're near 8012 11th Avenue to understand the changes.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
1.	DVP26-01 - 8012 11th Avenue 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - DVP 26-01 - Sketch1 and 2 2026-04-28.pdf
3.	Attachment 2 - DVP 26-01 - Permit 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Property and Liability Insurance Contracts to be Awarded (2026)
briefs.summary: The city is renewing its property and liability insurance contracts for 2026. This covers things like damage to city property and legal claims against the city. The city council needs to approve awarding these contracts.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
1.	2026 Insurance Program - Contract Renewals - 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Networking Software Licenses: Council Approval Needed
briefs.summary: A contract award (CA) requires Council approval for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. This ensures the purchase of necessary software is officially authorized.
briefs.action_required: Await Council's decision on the networking software licenses contract.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
1.	CA - Networking Software Licenses 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: New Civic Facility Fit-Out Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract will be awarded for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. This ensures the facility is properly equipped for its intended use.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
1.	CA - New Civic Facility Fit-Out 2026-04-28 ___.pdf
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

briefs.title: External Audit Services Contract Change Requires Council Approval
briefs.summary: A contract change for external audit services needs approval from the Council. This ensures that the city's financial records are properly reviewed by an outside firm. The purpose is to maintain transparency and accountability in financial reporting.
briefs.action_required: No action required. Stay informed about Council's decision.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
1.	CC - External Audit Services 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Contract History (External Audit Services) 2026-04-28 .pdf
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex: Council Approves Geotechnical Consulting Contract Change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a change to the contract for geotechnical (soil and rock) consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project. This ensures the project has the necessary expertise for ground-related issues.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
1.	CC - BLRC Geotechnical Consulting Services 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Contract History (BLRC Geotechnical Consulting Services) 2026-04-28 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Spring 2026 Community Grants: Application Approval Requested
briefs.summary: The city council is reviewing applications for the Spring 2026 Community Grant program. These grants support local initiatives in areas like recreation, food security, and community programs. Council approval is needed to finalize the grant recipients.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS

2.	Appendix A - Recreational Sports Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
3.	Appendix B - Operating Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
4.	Appendix C - Initiative Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
5.	Appendix D - Subsidized Use Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
6.	Appendix E - Food Security Grants 2026.04.28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Fees and Charges Bylaw for Various Services (Effective June & September 2026)
briefs.summary: Burnaby is changing some fees for city services. These changes are part of the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. Some fees will change starting June 1, 2026, and others will change starting September 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated fee schedules to understand any cost changes for your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby amended Schedule F fees take effect
Burnaby amended Schedule F3 fees take effect

briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
1.	FMC - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw – 2026 Mid-Year Amendments 2026-04-28_.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Amendments to Schedule F, Effective June 1, 2026 2026-04-28.pdf
3.	Attachment 2 - Amendments to Schedule F3, Effective Septembr 1, 2026 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council Considers New Tenant Protection Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby is considering a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. It aims to give renters more security and rights. The council is reviewing the proposed bylaw and related changes to existing rules.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the bylaw's progress and potential impact on your rental properties.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
1.	PDC - Proposed Tenant Protection Framework 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 2026-04-28.pdf
3.	Attachment 2 - Key Terms and Changes between TAP and the proposed Burnaby TPB 2026-04-28.pdf
4.	Attachment 3 - Proposed Amendments to Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2026-04-28.pdf
5.	Attachment 4 - List of in-progress Rezoning Applications subject to TAP 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. The project aims to improve the bikeway for cyclists.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
1.	TC - Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project - Proposed Improvements - 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Project Scope - Southeast Bikeway 2026-04-28.pdf
3.	Attachment 2 - Southeast Area Proposed Improvements (bikeway) 2026-04-28 .pdf
4.	Attachment 3 - Big Bend Area Proposed Cross-Sections (bikeway) 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Improve Road Safety with Protected Intersection Design Standards
briefs.summary: Burnaby is updating its Town Centre Design Standards to include protected intersection geometry. This change aims to make roads safer for pedestrians and cyclists of all ages and abilities. Protected intersections separate cyclists and pedestrians from vehicle traffic, reducing the risk of accidents.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated design standards to understand how they affect your projects.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
1.	TC - Proposed Amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Exempting Some Brentwood Park Properties from Housing Density Rules
briefs.summary: Burnaby is considering whether to exempt some properties in the Brentwood Park area from new provincial rules that allow for more housing density. These properties have restrictive covenants (agreements that limit how the land can be used) or statutory building schemes (sets of rules about how buildings must look and be arranged).
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council decisions regarding Brentwood Park property exemptions.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
1.	Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schemes 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

briefs.title: Building Permit Activity Report Published by City
briefs.summary: The city published a report about building permits issued in March 2026. The report gives the City Council information about construction happening in the city.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
1.	SU - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Burnaby's 2025 Solid Waste and Recycling Report Released
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has released its 2025 Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) Annual Report. The report summarizes how well the city's recycling and waste collection systems performed. It gives Burnaby City Council an overview of the city's progress in this area.
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
1.	EC - Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf
2.	Attachment 1 - Solid Waste and Recycling 2025 Annual Report 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Increase Support for Local Food Sustainability
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is working to make the local food system more sustainable. This includes supporting urban farming, reducing food waste, and making sure everyone has access to healthy food. The city wants to create a food system that is good for the environment and the community.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about new initiatives and potential opportunities for your business.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
1.	EC - SU Enhancing Food Sustainability in Burnaby 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Burnaby: Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection to Get Safety Upgrades
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby plans to improve safety at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade entrance. These changes aim to make the area safer for drivers and pedestrians.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about construction and traffic changes.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
1.	TC - Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection Safety Improvements - 2026-04-28.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning bylaw amended for Grand Promenade, Austin Road, and Gatineau Place
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended Zoning Bylaw 14808. The changes affect the area around Grand Promenade, Austin Road, and Gatineau Place. The amendment (Bylaw No. 7, 2026) alters a previous rezoning (REZ #18-32). The changes include replacing some office space with a hotel and restaurant, adding 362 market strata units, and transferring the inclusionary housing requirement to the Carrigan site.
briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Construction & Development
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
1.	14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning approved for Kingsway mixed-use development
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved rezoning for 6535 Kingsway. Bylaw 14809 allows a 12-story building with 290 homes, commercial space at street level, and underground parking. The plan follows the City's official community plan and the Edmonds Community Plan.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
1.	14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535 Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.
(Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Zoning Change for Self-Storage on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a change to its zoning rules (Bylaw #14810) for a property at 2757 Norland Avenue. The change allows the construction of a five-story self-storage building. This follows the city's official community plan.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
1.	14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends E-Bike Bylaw: New Pilot Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its Street and Traffic Bylaw to include a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This means new rules will be in place for how and where these devices can be used within the city.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand the new rules for e-bikes and scooters.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
1.	14811.Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961 Amendment Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby allows e-bikes and electric kick scooters in parks (pilot program)
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its Parks Regulation Bylaw to allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in some parks. This is a pilot program to test how these vehicles work in park settings. The changes took effect in April 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand where e-bikes are permitted.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
1.	14812. Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amend Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Parks: E-bikes and Electric Kick Scooters Now Under Bylaw Enforcement
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to include rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters in parks. This means bylaw officers can now issue tickets for violations related to these devices in Burnaby parks.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated bylaws for e-bikes and scooters in parks.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
1.	14813 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw No. 3, 2026 BBY Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 .pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item
7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)

briefs.title: Burnaby Sets 2026 Property Tax Rates
briefs.summary: Burnaby has set the property tax rates for 2026. This bylaw decides how much tax you will pay on your land and any buildings on it. These rates are needed for the city to collect money under the Community Charter (a law that lets local governments raise money).
briefs.action_required: Review your 2026 property tax bill from the City of Burnaby for accuracy.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Construction & Development, Grocery & Food Retail, Cannabis, Liquor, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture, Labour & Employment, Professional Services, Spa & Wellness
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
1.	14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026)
Subject to approval of item 7.3.
